chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. NO.S-462 OF 2025 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ Date Order with Signature(s) of Judge(s) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- D/O MATTER 1 FOR ORDERS ON CMA. NO.4420/2025 2. FOR ORDERS ON CMA. NO.4444/2025 28.07.2025 Mr. Qamar Hussain Sadozai, Advocate for the Petitioner alongwith Petitioner Mrs. Faiza Parekh Mr. Muhammad Bux Advocate for the Respondent alongwith Respondent Uzair Saleem ------------------------- Through this application (CMA No.4420/2025), the respondent seeks review of the consent order dated 05.06.2025 on the ground that he is a cancer patient and due to his critical health condition he has suffered losses in his business and is facing financial crisis. Counter affidavit to the application has been filed by the petitioner challenging the maintainability of the review application against the Consent Order. On 10.07.2025, the respondent counsel also filed a Statement, confirming therein that the consent order dated 05.06.2025 has been partially complied with to the extent of deposit of passports and meetings with the minors. Today, both parties are present in person along with their respective counsel and the matter has been heard in detail, whereby case for review under section 114 or under Order 47 CPC., is not made out. However, during course of hearing, both parties and their counsels submit that they would be satisfied, if the review Application is disposed-of and the Order dated 05.06.2025 is modified in the following terms: i.

Today, both parties are present in person along with their respective counsel and the matter has been heard in detail, whereby case for review under section 114 or under Order 47 CPC., is not made out. However, during course of hearing, both parties and their counsels submit that they would be satisfied, if the review Application is disposed-of and the Order dated 05.06.2025 is modified in the following terms: i. The passports of all three minors have already been deposited by the respondent with the learned Guardian Court in compliance of the consent order dated 05.06.2025; ii. Both the parties further confirm that in compliance of the consent order dated 05.06.2025, custody of two minors aged around 10 years was temporarily handed over to the respondent for one week from 08.06.2025 to 14.06.2025, whereas the third female minor aged about five years is also regularly meeting on every Saturday and Sunday. iii. Within three days, the respondent shall directly pay the outstanding school fees of all three minors in their respective schools, in compliance of the consent order dated 05.06.2025; iv. The respondent shall deposit the past outstanding monthly maintenance of the minors for the period February 2025 till July 2025, at the rate as already ordered by the learned Guardian Court, within 60 days from today. v. That from August 2025 onwards, the respondent shall pay before the Guardian court monthly maintenance of the minors at the rate of Rs.20,000 per minor, in addition to the monthly school fees of each minor which the respondent shall continue to deposit directly in their respective schools. vi. Subject to the above, periodic meetings between the minors and the respondent shall continue. The applications are therefore disposed of in terms of the above order which has been passed by consent and in presence of both the petitioner and respondent and their respective counsels. JUDGE MUSHARRAF ALI
